meaning: adv.离开(家乡),在战场上,在田野
examples: Journalists came from as far afield as China.
Some of the demand for the services and goods was no doubt fulfilled by people drawn from far afield, but some local people certainly became entwined in this new economy.
You can hire a car if you want to explore further afield.
The farthest afield XTO has ventured is Alaska, where the geology is similar to that of west Texas.
Now, they are enjoying world-wide fame and have fan clubs as far afield as California.
